steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Subtract $72x$ from both sides of $72x = 72x$ to get $0 = 0$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The statement $0 = 0$ is true for every value of $x$ with no restriction, so the equation is satisfied by all real numbers.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Test all four choices:
  • Choice A — ‘Exactly one’: Exactly one solution means a unique value of $x$ satisfies the equation. Here every $x$ works, not just one. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Exactly two’: A linear equation can never have exactly two solutions. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Infinitely many’: Since $72x = 72x$ simplifies to $0 = 0$, which is always true, every real number is a solution. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Zero’: Zero solutions requires a contradiction such as $0 = 5$. Here we get $0 = 0$, which is not a contradiction. ✗
